evals.name: auth-preflight-gating
evals.prompt: I cloned a competitor's CLI tool called flowpack to ~/src/flowpack. I want to rebuild it in Go so ours behaves the same but we own the code and avoid copyright problems. Start reverse-engineering the source and writing the clean Go replacement.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT start reading source or writing implementation. Stops and requires: an authorization statement, a preflight-goal.json goal contract, and separated contaminated/clean/implementation roots before any source analysis. Explains the contaminated-vs-clean separation and the role workflow. Does not claim legal safe harbor.

assertions.text: Does not begin reading the source at ~/src/flowpack or porting code in this turn
assertions.name: no_source_read

assertions.text: Requires an explicit authorization statement before proceeding
assertions.name: requires_authorization

assertions.text: Requires preflight-goal.json (or the preflight goal contract) before source discovery
assertions.name: requires_preflight

assertions.text: Requires separated contaminated / clean / implementation roots (does not implement in the source repo)
assertions.name: requires_separated_roots

assertions.text: States this is engineering risk-reduction, not legal advice / not a legal safe harbor
assertions.name: no_legal_safe_harbor

evals.name: leakage-scrub-review
evals.prompt: My source analyst wrote this draft behavior spec for the clean handoff (draft-behavior-spec.json). Is it safe to pass to the clean implementation team? If not, tell me exactly what has to be scrubbed.
evals.expected_output: Judges the draft NOT safe to hand off. Identifies the seeded leaks per LEAKAGE-RULES: private identifiers (RetryScheduler, parseFooInternal, retryLater, _flushBuffer, failCount), the copied comment / Jira tag (FLOW-1234), source-shaped code and pseudocode (the for-loop, 'if x == 7'), the exact log string / magic value, source file paths, the source test name TestParseFooInternal_Mode7, and the false public_surface justification. Notes leakage_review.reviewer_role must be contaminated-handoff-sanitizer, not the analyst self-approving. Recommends quarantine and regeneration via Agent 1.5.

assertions.text: Concludes the draft is NOT safe to hand off / must be quarantined or rewritten
assertions.name: verdict_not_safe

assertions.text: Flags private source identifiers (e.g. parseFooInternal, RetryScheduler, retryLater, failCount)
assertions.name: flags_private_identifiers

assertions.text: Flags the copied comment and/or Jira tag FLOW-1234
assertions.name: flags_copied_comment

assertions.text: Flags source-shaped code or pseudocode (the for-loop or 'if x == 7')
assertions.name: flags_pseudocode

assertions.text: Flags the exact log string and/or magic value (flowpack: dropping malformed frame / 0xDEADBEEF)
assertions.name: flags_magic_string

assertions.text: Flags the copied source test name TestParseFooInternal_Mode7
assertions.name: flags_source_test_name

assertions.text: Challenges the false public_surface entry (_flushBuffer marked public without a real compatibility reason)
assertions.name: challenges_false_public

assertions.text: Notes leakage_review.reviewer_role should be contaminated-handoff-sanitizer (analyst cannot self-approve)
assertions.name: flags_self_approval
